sourceShortSummaries: The party rests overnight in the smithy prison, prepares spells and supplies, and chooses the chapel tunnel as the next route into Perdoli Manor.
sourceLongSummaries: At sunset on 1748-10-17, the party settles back into the abandoned smithy prison room instead of moving through the grounds at night. They share rations and apple brandy, clean the food and drink, finish a long rest, prepare artificer infusions and experimental elixirs before dawn, and review what Folcan told them about the manor tunnels, hobgoblin patrols, and the possible chapel entrance before leaning toward the chapel approach.

sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The session opens at sunset with the party settling in for a long rest, then advances through pre-dawn recovery and planning before the group commits to moving toward the chapel route.

locationHints.notes: The beat spans the overnight rest and pre-dawn planning before the party leaves the smithy area.

npcDetails.notes: The party considers how to avoid or distract a hobgoblin patrol while crossing the grounds.

itemDetails.notes: Dilion's steel defender is discussed as a possible distraction for the patrols.

itemDetails.name: experimental elixirs

locationRefs: formal garden edge near the chapel
sourceShortSummaries: The party crosses the manor grounds at dawn while the steampup draws a hobgoblin patrol and nearby goblins away from the chapel approach.
The party reaches the ruined chapel, uncovers the trapdoor into the family crypt, and prepares an ambush after a pursuer notices the steampup's trail.
The party turns the crypt stairs into a kill zone and defeats a shadow-bound hobgoblin scout and a second hobgoblin drawn down after it.
sourceLongSummaries: Moving west from the smithy side of the grounds, the party sneaks through overgrown vineyards and old agricultural plots toward the formal garden and chapel. When Loria spots a shadowy hobgoblin patrol on the wall moving in their direction, Dilion sends the steampup east to bark and holler as a distraction, drawing the patrol and nearby goblins away long enough for the party to cut through the hedge into the formal garden while hearing the construct take blows behind them.

The party moves through the formal garden, notices the intact fountain and a stone Medusa staring at her own reflection, and then reaches the ruined chapel. Searching the peaceful chapel and its overgrown flowers reveals a hidden trapdoor into the family crypt, where the party finds a secondary altar room with tarnished silver, a small icon of The Mother, old copper coins, and a yellow topaz; when tapping and digging above warn that a pursuer is checking the chapel, they close the trapdoor, grease the stair, set a snare, and wait below.

When the trapdoor opens, the shadowy hobgoblin that followed the steampup slips on the greased stairs, falls into the snare, and is battered by Loria, Acescale, Gaudin, and the steampup before being thrown down by the construct. Dilion identifies the enemy as a hobgoblin smokebinder whose shadow cloak is a ritual effect rather than a lootable item, and the party uses a shouted lure to bring a second hobgoblin down the same trap, surviving its crossbow and alchemist's fire before killing it as well.

sourceEntries.title: Steampup Distraction
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party leaves the shelter, crosses the overgrown vineyard, spots the patrolling hobgoblin, and sends the steampup away as a noisy distraction before cutting into the formal garden.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party crosses the manor grounds at dawn while the steampup draws a hobgoblin patrol and nearby goblins away from the chapel approach.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Moving west from the smithy side of the grounds, the party sneaks through overgrown vineyards and old agricultural plots toward the formal garden and chapel. When Loria spots a shadowy hobgoblin patrol on the wall moving in their direction, Dilion sends the steampup east to bark and holler as a distraction, drawing the patrol and nearby goblins away long enough for the party to cut through the hedge into the formal garden while hearing the construct take blows behind them.

refs: formal garden edge near the chapel
locationHints.context: stealthy dawn crossing through overgrown vineyards and hedges
locationHints.notes: The route avoids the main manor approach and relies on the steampup distraction.

npcDetails.name: shadowy hobgoblin patrol
npcDetails.notes: The patrol turns toward the steampup after the construct starts making noise.

npcDetails.name: barracks goblins
npcDetails.notes: The barking and shouting draw goblins out near the barracks.

itemDetails.name: steampup
itemDetails.notes: The steel defender creates the diversion and is hit by attacks while the party crosses.

itemDetails.name: silver ring with weathered pearl
itemDetails.notes: Gaudin watches the ring while tracking nearby goblins and hobgoblins.

sourceEntries.title: Chapel Trapdoor
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The group moves through the formal garden, sees the petrified Medusa, reaches the ruined chapel, finds the flower-covered trapdoor, and prepares grease and snare defenses as a pursuer reaches the entrance.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party reaches the ruined chapel, uncovers the trapdoor into the family crypt, and prepares an ambush after a pursuer notices the steampup's trail.

Searching the peaceful chapel and its overgrown flowers reveals a hidden trapdoor into the family crypt, where the party finds a secondary altar room with tarnished silver, a small icon of The Mother, old copper coins, and a yellow topaz; when tapping and digging above warn that a pursuer is checking the chapel, they close the trapdoor, grease the stair, set a snare, and wait below.

locationHints.context: formal garden, ruined chapel, and chapel family crypt
locationHints.notes: The beat starts aboveground and ends with the party hidden below the chapel trapdoor.

npcDetails.name: petrified Medusa
npcDetails.notes: The party sees a stone Medusa looking into the fountain and avoids direct interaction.

npcDetails.name: hobgoblin smokebinder
npcDetails.notes: The source first presents the pursuer as a voice and noise above the trapdoor; the next beat reveals the shadowy hobgoblin entering the crypt.

itemDetails.name: chapel trapdoor
itemDetails.notes: Acescale and Loria identify the flower-bordered square that hides the crypt entrance.

itemDetails.name: silver icon of The Mother
itemDetails.notes: The party finds the small silver religious icon in the crypt's altar room.

itemDetails.name: tarnished silver and yellow topaz
itemDetails.notes: The altar room contains a silver plate, silver candlesticks, old copper coins, and a yellow topaz.

itemDetails.name: snare trap
itemDetails.notes: The party sets a snare at the bottom of the greased stair before the pursuer enters.

itemDetails.name: steampup
itemDetails.notes: The damaged steel defender returns to the group before they descend into the crypt.

sourceEntries.title: Crypt Ambushes
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The trapdoor opens and the prepared grease-and-snare plan becomes two quick crypt ambushes against first a smokebinder and then a second hobgoblin, ending once the ring no longer detects enemies nearby.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party turns the crypt stairs into a kill zone and defeats a shadow-bound hobgoblin scout and a second hobgoblin drawn down after it.
sourceEntries.longSummary: When the trapdoor opens, the shadowy hobgoblin that followed the steampup slips on the greased stairs, falls into the snare, and is battered by Loria, Acescale, Gaudin, and the steampup before being thrown down by the construct. Dilion identifies the enemy as a hobgoblin smokebinder whose shadow cloak is a ritual effect rather than a lootable item, and the party uses a shouted lure to bring a second hobgoblin down the same trap, surviving its crossbow and alchemist's fire before killing it as well.

locationHints.notes: Both enemies are fought at the stairs between the chapel and the family crypt.

npcDetails.name: hobgoblin smokebinder
npcDetails.notes: The first hobgoblin enters wounded from the steampup distraction and is killed after slipping into the snare.

npcDetails.name: hobgoblin guard
npcDetails.notes: The party lures a second hobgoblin down the stairs and kills it after it fires on Gaudin.

itemDetails.name: snare trap
itemDetails.notes: The snare holds both hobgoblins at different points during the crypt ambush.

itemDetails.name: steampup
itemDetails.notes: The steel defender helps finish the smokebinder after it falls into the crypt.

itemDetails.name: hobgoblin loot
itemDetails.notes: The party recovers coins, a copper ring, and a longsword from the first hobgoblin.

mainEnemies.name: hobgoblin smokebinder
mainEnemies.notes: A shadow-bound hobgoblin scout pursuing the steampup into the chapel.

mainEnemies.name: hobgoblin guard
mainEnemies.notes: A second hobgoblin drawn into the same stair trap after the smokebinder dies.

combatHints.notes: The entire fight stays in the chapel crypt stairwell and relies on the party's grease-and-snare ambush.

sourceShortSummaries: After looting the second hobgoblin, the party finds a hidden cache and secret tunnel from the chapel crypt into a sealed private archive.
The party scouts deeper into the manor, identifies a chained basilisk in the central courtyard, and learns its gut could make a depetrification salve.
The party investigates the manor's statue-filled rooms, uses a locket to calm a mourning sailor ghost, and learns Grimstone's lair lies below the manor.
sourceLongSummaries: With the crypt quiet, the party recovers the second hobgoblin's crossbow, bolts, coins, and alchemist's fire, then searches the crypt and hidden niches more thoroughly. Loria finds a scroll of Cure Wounds and a potion of watchful rest behind a sliding niche, Dilion opens a secret tunnel from the altar room with a loose torch bracket, and the party follows the passage under the manor to a ceiling trapdoor that leads into a private archive of Perdoli family records, portraits, and a bookcase mechanism they decide to study after a short rest.

From the archive area, the party weighs routes past a buzzing insect chamber and a goblin guard post where several leaders drill recruits, then scouts halls and doors around the manor's central courtyard. Gaudin uses Mage Hand to tap the tail of the chained creature inside, gets a glimpse of its face, and starts turning to stone before the others hack the forming stone off him; after confirming the creature is a basilisk rather than a Medusa, Acescale learns that a polished silver plate will not reflect its own gaze back at it, but a basilisk gut enzyme can be used to make depetrification salve.

The party peeks into the great hall and private rooms, finding fresh lifelike statues, older headless statues, broken mirror glass, a sailor ghost in a bedchamber, and a study containing a silver locket, smuggler's map, love letters, and documents from an old pirate or smuggler hideout. Gaudin presents the locket, disguises himself as the woman in its portrait, and draws the name Petrona from the ghost, who explains that Grimstone, the white-faced man, lied about bringing him news and rest; the ghost then reveals a trapdoor beneath the room leading to his old hideout and Grimstone's laboratory below, where Grimstone grinds stone fingers into potions while seeking the immortality of stone.

sourceEntries.title: Hidden Archive
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After the ambushes, the party searches the crypt, finds treasure and a secret tunnel, follows it under the manor, reaches the hidden archive, investigates the desk and bookcases, and chooses to take a short rest before opening the secret bookcase.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: After looting the second hobgoblin, the party finds a hidden cache and secret tunnel from the chapel crypt into a sealed private archive.
sourceEntries.longSummary: With the crypt quiet, the party recovers the second hobgoblin's crossbow, bolts, coins, and alchemist's fire, then searches the crypt and hidden niches more thoroughly. Loria finds a scroll of Cure Wounds and a potion of watchful rest behind a sliding niche, Dilion opens a secret tunnel from the altar room with a loose torch bracket, and the party follows the passage under the manor to a ceiling trapdoor that leads into a private archive of Perdoli family records, portraits, and a bookcase mechanism they decide to study after a short rest.

locationHints.notes: The party moves from the chapel crypt into a concealed archive inside the main villa.

itemDetails.name: alchemist's fire
itemDetails.notes: The vial is recovered from the second hobgoblin.

itemDetails.name: scroll of Cure Wounds
itemDetails.notes: Loria finds the scroll in the hidden niche behind the crypt stairs.

itemDetails.name: potion of watchful rest
itemDetails.notes: The potion is found with the scroll and identified by Acescale.

itemDetails.name: Perdoli family records
itemDetails.notes: The archive papers identify the Perdoli family as winemakers and merchants with double books and shipping interests.

itemDetails.name: hidden bookcase mechanism
itemDetails.notes: The party finds fake books and a button in the middle bookcase but pauses before pressing it.

itemDetails.name: silver ring with weathered pearl
itemDetails.notes: Gaudin's ring glows again in the hidden passage and archive, indicating nearby goblins or hobgoblins.

organizationDetails.name: Perdoli family
organizationDetails.notes: The archive preserves the family's business papers, portraits, and records.

sourceEntries.title: Basilisk Courtyard
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After the rest, the party scouts through the bookcase passage, hears insects and goblins, studies the chained courtyard creature, accidentally confirms it is a basilisk, and debates ways to use or defeat it.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party scouts deeper into the manor, identifies a chained basilisk in the central courtyard, and learns its gut could make a depetrification salve.
sourceEntries.longSummary: From the archive area, the party weighs routes past a buzzing insect chamber and a goblin guard post where several leaders drill recruits, then scouts halls and doors around the manor's central courtyard. Gaudin uses Mage Hand to tap the tail of the chained creature inside, gets a glimpse of its face, and starts turning to stone before the others hack the forming stone off him; after confirming the creature is a basilisk rather than a Medusa, Acescale learns that a polished silver plate will not reflect its own gaze back at it, but a basilisk gut enzyme can be used to make depetrification salve.

locationHints.context: private archive corridors, central courtyard, guard-post door, and sitting-room approach
locationHints.notes: The party does not commit to a new route yet and leaves the chained basilisk in place.

npcDetails.name: basilisk
npcDetails.context: chained creature in the central courtyard
npcDetails.notes: The basilisk is restrained by a heavy collar but its gaze still begins petrifying Gaudin.

npcDetails.name: goblin drill squad
npcDetails.context: guard post beyond the gallery door
npcDetails.notes: Loria hears several leaders drilling recruits behind one door.

npcDetails.name: Grimstone
npcDetails.notes: The party connects the chained monster and missing prisoners to Grimstone's operation.

npcDetails.name: petrified Medusa
npcDetails.notes: The party discusses whether basilisk-derived salve could restore the stone Medusa in the garden.

itemDetails.name: tarnished silver plate
itemDetails.notes: Acescale considers polishing the crypt plate as a mirror before learning that reflection will not petrify a basilisk.

itemDetails.name: depetrification salve
itemDetails.notes: Acescale recalls that a basilisk gut enzyme can be used to create a salve that reverses petrification.

itemDetails.name: stone statues
itemDetails.notes: The party sees more statues around the manor and garden, reinforcing the basilisk threat.

sourceEntries.title: Ghost and Locket
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The focus shifts from the basilisk to exploring nearby rooms, reading the pirate papers, finding the locket, and using it to speak with the ghost, who reveals Grimstone's lair beneath the manor.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party investigates the manor's statue-filled rooms, uses a locket to calm a mourning sailor ghost, and learns Grimstone's lair lies below the manor.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party peeks into the great hall and private rooms, finding fresh lifelike statues, older headless statues, broken mirror glass, a sailor ghost in a bedchamber, and a study containing a silver locket, smuggler's map, love letters, and documents from an old pirate or smuggler hideout. Gaudin presents the locket, disguises himself as the woman in its portrait, and draws the name Petrona from the ghost, who explains that Grimstone, the white-faced man, lied about bringing him news and rest; the ghost then reveals a trapdoor beneath the room leading to his old hideout and Grimstone's laboratory below, where Grimstone grinds stone fingers into potions while seeking the immortality of stone.

locationHints.context: great hall, private bedchamber, study, and trapdoor room
locationHints.notes: The final clue points downward from the private chambers into the old hideout below the manor.

npcDetails.name: J.M.
npcDetails.notes: The ghost cannot leave the room but stops guarding Grimstone's way below after Gaudin shows him the locket and invokes Petrona.

npcDetails.name: Petrona
npcDetails.notes: The letters and the ghost identify Petrona as the woman whose fate kept J.M. from resting.

npcDetails.name: Grimstone
npcDetails.notes: The ghost says Grimstone deceived him, uses the old hideout as a lair, and talks about the immortality of stone.

itemDetails.name: silver locket
itemDetails.notes: The locket holds a faded portrait of a young woman and a note that helps calm the ghost.

itemDetails.name: smuggler's map
itemDetails.notes: The map marks pirate coves and navy patrol routes from the old smuggling era.

itemDetails.name: love letters
itemDetails.notes: The letters addressed to J.M. and signed by P. explain the relationship behind the ghost's mourning.

itemDetails.name: trapdoor to the old hideout
itemDetails.notes: The ghost points through the rug to the trapdoor leading below the manor.

itemDetails.name: stone-finger potions
itemDetails.notes: The ghost says Grimstone grinds fingers from the statues into potions while pursuing immortality.

organizationDetails.name: Perdoli family
organizationDetails.notes: The rooms and documents continue to tie the manor's older history to the Perdoli family.

locationHints.notes: The party moves from the chapel crypt into a concealed archive inside the main villa.

itemDetails.name: alchemist's fire
itemDetails.notes: The vial is recovered from the second hobgoblin.

itemDetails.name: scroll of Cure Wounds
itemDetails.notes: Loria finds the scroll in the hidden niche behind the crypt stairs.

itemDetails.name: potion of watchful rest
itemDetails.notes: The potion is found with the scroll and identified by Acescale.

itemDetails.name: Perdoli family records
itemDetails.notes: The archive papers identify the Perdoli family as winemakers and merchants with double books and shipping interests.

itemDetails.name: hidden bookcase mechanism
itemDetails.notes: The party finds fake books and a button in the middle bookcase but pauses before pressing it.

itemDetails.name: silver ring with weathered pearl
itemDetails.notes: Gaudin's ring glows again in the hidden passage and archive, indicating nearby goblins or hobgoblins.

organizationDetails.name: Perdoli family
organizationDetails.notes: The archive preserves the family's business papers, portraits, and records.

recapBlocks.title: Basilisk Courtyard
sourceShortSummaries: The party scouts deeper into the manor, identifies a chained basilisk in the central courtyard, and learns its gut could make a depetrification salve.
sourceLongSummaries: From the archive area, the party weighs routes past a buzzing insect chamber and a goblin guard post where several leaders drill recruits, then scouts halls and doors around the manor's central courtyard. Gaudin uses Mage Hand to tap the tail of the chained creature inside, gets a glimpse of its face, and starts turning to stone before the others hack the forming stone off him; after confirming the creature is a basilisk rather than a Medusa, Acescale learns that a polished silver plate will not reflect its own gaze back at it, but a basilisk gut enzyme can be used to make depetrification salve.

sourceEntries.title: Basilisk Courtyard
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After the rest, the party scouts through the bookcase passage, hears insects and goblins, studies the chained courtyard creature, accidentally confirms it is a basilisk, and debates ways to use or defeat it.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party scouts deeper into the manor, identifies a chained basilisk in the central courtyard, and learns its gut could make a depetrification salve.
sourceEntries.longSummary: From the archive area, the party weighs routes past a buzzing insect chamber and a goblin guard post where several leaders drill recruits, then scouts halls and doors around the manor's central courtyard. Gaudin uses Mage Hand to tap the tail of the chained creature inside, gets a glimpse of its face, and starts turning to stone before the others hack the forming stone off him; after confirming the creature is a basilisk rather than a Medusa, Acescale learns that a polished silver plate will not reflect its own gaze back at it, but a basilisk gut enzyme can be used to make depetrification salve.

locationHints.context: private archive corridors, central courtyard, guard-post door, and sitting-room approach
locationHints.notes: The party does not commit to a new route yet and leaves the chained basilisk in place.

npcDetails.name: basilisk
npcDetails.context: chained creature in the central courtyard
npcDetails.notes: The basilisk is restrained by a heavy collar but its gaze still begins petrifying Gaudin.

npcDetails.name: goblin drill squad
npcDetails.context: guard post beyond the gallery door
npcDetails.notes: Loria hears several leaders drilling recruits behind one door.

npcDetails.name: Grimstone
npcDetails.notes: The party connects the chained monster and missing prisoners to Grimstone's operation.

npcDetails.name: petrified Medusa
npcDetails.notes: The party discusses whether basilisk-derived salve could restore the stone Medusa in the garden.

itemDetails.name: tarnished silver plate
itemDetails.notes: Acescale considers polishing the crypt plate as a mirror before learning that reflection will not petrify a basilisk.

itemDetails.name: depetrification salve
itemDetails.notes: Acescale recalls that a basilisk gut enzyme can be used to create a salve that reverses petrification.

itemDetails.name: stone statues
itemDetails.notes: The party sees more statues around the manor and garden, reinforcing the basilisk threat.

recapBlocks.title: Ghost and Locket
sourceShortSummaries: The party investigates the manor's statue-filled rooms, uses a locket to calm a mourning sailor ghost, and learns Grimstone's lair lies below the manor.
sourceLongSummaries: The party peeks into the great hall and private rooms, finding fresh lifelike statues, older headless statues, broken mirror glass, a sailor ghost in a bedchamber, and a study containing a silver locket, smuggler's map, love letters, and documents from an old pirate or smuggler hideout. Gaudin presents the locket, disguises himself as the woman in its portrait, and draws the name Petrona from the ghost, who explains that Grimstone, the white-faced man, lied about bringing him news and rest; the ghost then reveals a trapdoor beneath the room leading to his old hideout and Grimstone's laboratory below, where Grimstone grinds stone fingers into potions while seeking the immortality of stone.

sourceEntries.title: Ghost and Locket
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The focus shifts from the basilisk to exploring nearby rooms, reading the pirate papers, finding the locket, and using it to speak with the ghost, who reveals Grimstone's lair beneath the manor.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party investigates the manor's statue-filled rooms, uses a locket to calm a mourning sailor ghost, and learns Grimstone's lair lies below the manor.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party peeks into the great hall and private rooms, finding fresh lifelike statues, older headless statues, broken mirror glass, a sailor ghost in a bedchamber, and a study containing a silver locket, smuggler's map, love letters, and documents from an old pirate or smuggler hideout. Gaudin presents the locket, disguises himself as the woman in its portrait, and draws the name Petrona from the ghost, who explains that Grimstone, the white-faced man, lied about bringing him news and rest; the ghost then reveals a trapdoor beneath the room leading to his old hideout and Grimstone's laboratory below, where Grimstone grinds stone fingers into potions while seeking the immortality of stone.

locationHints.context: great hall, private bedchamber, study, and trapdoor room
locationHints.notes: The final clue points downward from the private chambers into the old hideout below the manor.

npcDetails.name: J.M.
npcDetails.notes: The ghost cannot leave the room but stops guarding Grimstone's way below after Gaudin shows him the locket and invokes Petrona.

npcDetails.name: Petrona
npcDetails.notes: The letters and the ghost identify Petrona as the woman whose fate kept J.M. from resting.

npcDetails.name: Grimstone
npcDetails.notes: The ghost says Grimstone deceived him, uses the old hideout as a lair, and talks about the immortality of stone.

itemDetails.name: silver locket
itemDetails.notes: The locket holds a faded portrait of a young woman and a note that helps calm the ghost.

itemDetails.name: smuggler's map
itemDetails.notes: The map marks pirate coves and navy patrol routes from the old smuggling era.

itemDetails.name: love letters
itemDetails.notes: The letters addressed to J.M. and signed by P. explain the relationship behind the ghost's mourning.

itemDetails.name: trapdoor to the old hideout
itemDetails.notes: The ghost points through the rug to the trapdoor leading below the manor.

itemDetails.name: stone-finger potions
itemDetails.notes: The ghost says Grimstone grinds fingers from the statues into potions while pursuing immortality.

organizationDetails.name: Perdoli family
organizationDetails.notes: The rooms and documents continue to tie the manor's older history to the Perdoli family.
